title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.012
question_text: Susan is the lead investigator for a security incident and realizes that she will not be able to complete her investigation without causing severe disruption to the business. The action she feels she must take exceeds the authority granted to her under the incident response plan. What should Susan do?

answer_choices.label: A. Consult with higher levels of management
B. Discount the action as a possibility because it exceeds her authority
C. Shut down all business operations immediately until she develops a plan
D. Take the action immediately to protect the business

correct_answer_label: A. Consult with higher levels of management
explanation: Susan should bring the decision to managers with the authority to authorize the activity and get their input. She should not take drastic actions on her own authority. She should only take actions that are based on a sound analysis and are within the scope of her authority.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.013
question_text: Which one of the following investigation types has the highest standard of evidence?
explanation: Criminal investigations may result in the imprisonment of individuals and, therefore, have the highest standard of evidence to protect the rights of the accused.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.014
question_text: Katie is planning to conduct a threat‐hunting initiative and is building a database of common attack signatures that she will search for on her organization’s systems. What term best describes the type of information is Katie gathering?
explanation: This question is a little tricky because several answers may be partially correct. Katie may be using open source intelligence (OSINT) sources to collect this information. She may also have information about vulnerabilities or malware signatures in her collection effort. However, the term that best describes this information is indicators of compromise (IoC). IoC may include information about malware and vulnerabilities, but it may also include other indicators, such as log entries or IP addresses. IoC may come from OSINT sources, but it may also come from proprietary sources.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.015
question_text: Tom enables an application firewall provided by his cloud infrastructure as a service provider that is designed to block many types of application attacks. When viewed from a risk management perspective, what metric is Tom attempting to lower by implementing this countermeasure?
explanation: Installing a device that will block attacks is an attempt to lower risk by reducing the likelihood of a successful application attack.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.016
question_text: Rebecca is a security manager for a subsidiary of a multinational corporation. The larger organization’s policy requires the use of two‐factor authentication on all systems handling sensitive information, but a business case has arisen for disabling this control in one specific situation. What would be the best course of action for Rebecca?

answer_choices.label: A. Remove the two‐factor requirement if the business case justifies the action
B. Refuse the request because it violates policy
C. Escalate the issue to senior management
D. Switch to a different two‐factor mechanism

correct_answer_label: C. Escalate the issue to senior management
explanation: Rebecca should escalate the issue to senior management for resolution because it involves taking actions outside her scope of control. Changing systems is likely to be expensive and require management approval. There is also no evidence that this would resolve the situation. Rebecca does not have the authority to remove the two‐factor requirement on her own since it comes from the parent corporation. Refusing the request outright is not sensitive to business needs and would likely alienate the requestor.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.017
question_text: Renee is responding to a cybersecurity incident at her organization where the attackers used a botnet to disrupt access to the organization’s website, preventing legitimate customers from placing orders. What type of attack took place?
explanation: This is an example of a denial‐of‐service attack that disrupted the availability of the website for legitimate users.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.018
question_text: Which one of the following indicators suggests that a security standard may require modification?

answer_choices.label: A. The standard contains information that conflicts with security procedures.
B. An audit identified that many users are following practices that do not comply with the standard.
C. An audit discovered a control gap on systems that fully implemented the standard.
D. The standard contains information that conflicts with security guidelines.

correct_answer_label: C. An audit discovered a control gap on systems that fully implemented the standard.
explanation: Standards that do not achieve the organization’s control objectives should be modified to meet those objectives. Standards are of higher precedence than guidelines and procedures. If there is a conflict between the two, it most likely indicates that the guideline or procedure must be modified. If users are not complying with the standard, they should be educated on how to better meet their security responsibilities. This does not necessarily indicate needed change in the standard.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.019
question_text: Norm is conducting a penetration test and has gained access to an organization’s database server. He then creates a user account for himself on that system in the hope that he can use this account to access the system at a later date, even if the original exploit that he used is patched. What term best describes Norm’s activity?
explanation: Persistence includes any technique used to maintain access to a system, even after the attack is discovered. Norm is creating this user account for this purpose, so his activity is best described as persistence.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.020
question_text: Julie is developing a compensating control to help her organization mitigate the risk of downtime due to hardware failure. What control would best meet this need?

answer_choices.label: B. Backups in a secure location

explanation: Fault‐tolerant hardware will directly reduce the amount of downtime in the event of a hardware failure. Insurance may provide financial benefits in the event of a security incident but it will not reduce downtime. An incident notification plan may reduce the length of downtime, but this reduction is not likely to be significant. Backups will reduce the likelihood of data loss but will not likely have a significant impact on downtime.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.021
question_text: Which one of the following components of an organization’s information security program would you expect to contain the highest‐level information?
explanation: An information security governance program typically includes all of the crucial documents that define the organization’s security activities. Security strategies are the highest‐level documents listed here, as they contain the overall vision for the program. The other documents listed here contain more detailed guidance.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.022
question_text: Andrea is reviewing a draft of the Domer Doodads information security policy and finds that it contains the statement below. Which one of these statements would be more appropriately placed in a different document?

answer_choices.label: A. Domer Doodads considers cybersecurity and compliance to be of critical importance to the business.
B. Domer Doodads designates the Chief Information Security Officer as the individual with primary responsibility for information security.
C. The Chief Information Security Officer is granted the authority to create specific requirements that implement this policy.
D. All access to financial systems must use multifactor authentication for remote connections.

correct_answer_label: D. All access to financial systems must use multifactor authentication for remote connections.
explanation: It is normal to find statements in an information security policy that declare the importance of cybersecurity to the organization, designate a specific individual as responsible for the cybersecurity function, and grant that individual authority over cybersecurity. Specific requirements, such as requiring multifactor authentication for financial systems, would be more appropriately placed in a standard than a policy.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.023
question_text: Nora recently learned of a new cybersecurity threat facing her organization from a group of attackers exploiting buffer overflow vulnerabilities. She deployed a new set of rules on the organization’s web application firewall to block these attacks. What risk management strategy is Nora using?
explanation: Risk mitigation strategies take actions designed to reduce the likelihood and/or impact of a security threat. In this case, Nora deployed firewall rules that will attempt to block the attack, reducing their likelihood of success. Risk avoidance strategies seek to remove the source of the risk, such as removing the applications entirely. Risk acceptance acknowledges that the risk exists and takes no other action. Risk transference shifts some or all of the risk to a third party, such as an insurance carrier.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.024
question_text: Jeff is concerned about the effects that a ransomware attack might have on his organization and is designing a backup methodology that would allow the organization to quickly restore data after such an attack. What type of control is Jeff implementing?
explanation: Corrective controls remediate security issues that have already occurred. Restoring backups after a ransomware attack is an example of a corrective control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.025
question_text: What is the most important rule to follow when collecting evidence?

answer_choices.label: A. Transfer all equipment to a secure storage location.
B. List all people present while collecting evidence.
C. Do not turn off a computer until you photograph the screen.
D. Avoid the modification of evidence during the collection process.

correct_answer_label: D. Avoid the modification of evidence during the collection process.
explanation: Although the other options have some merit in individual cases, the most important rule is to never modify, or taint, evidence. If you modify evidence, it may be questioned by opposing attorneys and may even become inadmissible in court.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.026
question_text: Tim is a senior executive at a financial services firm, and he would like to ensure that the organization’s cybersecurity program is functioning effectively. What would be the best indicator for him to use as a measure of the program’s effectiveness?

answer_choices.label: A. Policies and procedures are documented
D. Audits do not contain repeat findings

explanation: This is a difficult question because all of these items are indicators of a healthy information security program. However, if Tim wanted to select one measure, the best one would be whether the program is meeting the organization’s control objectives. These objectives are the goals of the security program and would provide Tim with a big picture view of effectiveness. The other measures listed all cover specific areas and would not provide the same level of holistic view.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.027
question_text: Tom is the information security manager for a Fortune 500 company. A business manager recently approached him and asked him to approve an exception to the organization’s security procedures so that a critical business process may complete before an urgent deadline. What should Tom do first?

answer_choices.label: A. Deny the request because it violates the organization’s security procedures.
D. Approve an exception to the policy because the business need is urgent.

explanation: Tom should conduct a risk assessment of the situation that will determine whether compensating controls are appropriate. Tom should not approve an exception to the policy until after he conducts a risk assessment. Tom should not deny the request outright. He should perform a risk assessment and determine whether a compensating control is needed and possible. While Tom may end up deploying a compensating control, he should first conduct a risk assessment to guide the process.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.028
question_text: What pillar of information security describes the wholeness and completeness of information without unauthorized modifications?
explanation: Integrity ensures the wholeness and completeness of information and that it is not altered without authorization. Confidentiality ensures that sensitive information is not disclosed without authorization. Availability ensures that information and systems are available to authorized users when needed. Nonrepudiation ensures that the sender of a message cannot later disclaim authorship.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.029
question_text: Gina is working with leaders in her organization to implement a new information security program and is looking for a document that will help guide her in selecting the most appropriate objectives. Which one of the following documents would best assist her?
explanation: Gina should use a framework to help guide the objectives for her program. Common frameworks for cybersecurity programs include the Control Objectives for Information Technology (COBIT) and ISO 27001. The Gramm–Leach–Bliley Act (GLBA) and the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) are regulations affecting cybersecurity and should be addressed by the objectives but do not provide a comprehensive set of objectives. The Generally Accepted Privacy Principles (GAPP) are a framework for designing a privacy program, rather than a security program.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.030
question_text: Brian encountered a set of documents containing employee Social Security numbers and would like to replace those values with alternate values that are not sensitive but that may be restored using a lookup table. What type of data protection is Brian considering applying to this report?
explanation: Brian is considering replacing the Social Security numbers with tokens, using a tokenization technique. Masking would obscure portions of the numbers with alternate characters, such as Xs. Encryption and hashing would both apply algorithms to the values to convert them to non‐sensitive values, but do not replace them with values that may be found in a lookup table, as tokenization does.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.031
question_text: What security control can be used to clearly communicate to users the level of protection required for different data types?
explanation: Classification policies create different categories of data used within an organization and then specify the level of security control required for each classification level. Using classifications helps users understand the type of protection necessary for each data type they encounter.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.032
question_text: Barbara is updating the information security policy for her organization and needs to have it approved, but she is unsure what the appropriate approval process is for this type of document. Where should she turn to determine the correct process?
explanation: Barbara should be able to find the policy approval process in her organization’s information security charter. While other team members, such as internal/external auditors and help desk staff, may have information about the process, they are not the authoritative source for this information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.033
question_text: Brian would like to reduce the probability of a data breach that affects sensitive personal information. Which one of the following controls is most likely to achieve that objective?

answer_choices.label: B. Limiting the purposes for which data may be used
D. Minimizing the amount of data retained and the number of places where it is stored

correct_answer_label: D. Minimizing the amount of data retained and the number of places where it is stored
explanation: This question forces you to choose from several good options, as do many questions on the exam. We can rule out insurance because that does not alter the probability of a risk occurring. The remaining three options all do reduce the likelihood, but the best choice is minimizing the amount of data retained and the number of locations where it is stored, as this removes that data from the potential of a breach.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.034
question_text: What type of evidence must be authenticated by a witness who can uniquely identify it or through a documented chain of custody?
explanation: Real evidence must be either uniquely identified by a witness or authenticated through a documented chain of custody.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.035
question_text: What individual in an organization bears direct responsibility for ensuring the protection of business information through risk management, user education, control implementation, incident management, and related tasks?
explanation: The chief information security officer (CISO) is the individual appointed by organization leadership to have direct responsibility for information security matters. While the president or CEO of an organization does bear responsibility for everything that occurs within the organization, they are not directly responsible for information security activities. The CIO certainly plays an important role in information security but does not have direct responsibility for security issues. The CISO may or may not report to the CIO depending on the organization’s structure. The board chairperson is responsible for ensuring the effective operation of the organization’s governing body. That body should have oversight over information security matters but does not bear direct responsibility for action.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.036
question_text: Which one of the following disaster recovery test types provides the most thorough assessment of an organization’s ability to recover operations in the event of a disaster?
explanation: The full interruption test is the most effective way to test an organization’s disaster recovery capabilities because it simulates a real disaster. It is also the most dangerous type of test because it could disrupt operations if it fails. Parallel tests are a very effective test mechanism, but they are not as effective as full interruption tests. Structured walk‐throughs provide the organization with important insight into their disaster recovery capabilities, but they are not as effective as full interruption or parallel tests. During a checklist review, individuals review their disaster recovery responsibilities and provide input on the plan, but they do not gain the real‐world practical test results that you would gain from a parallel or full interruption test.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.037
question_text: What type of evidence refers to written documents that are brought into court to prove a fact?
explanation: Written documents brought into court to prove the facts of a case are referred to as documentary evidence. The best evidence rule states that when a document is used as evidence in a court proceeding, the original document must be introduced. The parol evidence rule states that when an agreement between parties is put into written form, the written document is assumed to contain all the terms of the agreement and no verbal agreements may modify the written agreement. Testimonial evidence is evidence consisting of the testimony of a witness, either verbal testimony in court or written testimony in a recorded deposition.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.038
question_text: Carla would like to “future proof” her organization’s security program against changes in the operating environment. As she performs this work, which of the following is not a common external influence that she should consider?
explanation: Carla should consider all of these items when assessing her organization’s preparedness for the future. However, the question asks about common external influences. Regulatory requirements, social media, and emerging technologies clearly fit into this category. Corporate policies, on the other hand, are an internal influence and would not be covered by this definition.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.039
question_text: Which one of the following is not one of the core goals of an information security program?
explanation: Authorization ensures that individuals can perform approved actions but are not able to exceed their own authority. While it is a security concept, it is not one of the three core goals of information security: confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.040
question_text: Harold conducts a review of his organization’s disaster recovery plan and realizes that the maximum tolerable outage for a service may be shorter than the organization can recover the service. What should he do next?

answer_choices.label: A. Notify the organization’s board of directors.
B. Conduct a business impact assessment.
C. Develop a plan to meet the MTO.

correct_answer_label: B. Conduct a business impact assessment.
explanation: Harold should first conduct a business impact assessment to determine whether the MTO and recovery time values are accurate and the impact on the business. Harold should not change the MTO without input from stakeholders and a business impact assessment. He may wind up developing a plan to meet the MTO, but he should only do so after conducting a business impact assessment. Harold should not immediately jump to notifying executives but rather develop a plan to determine whether the MTO is appropriate and/or modifications to the disaster recovery plan can bring the plan into acceptable parameters.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.041
question_text: Which one of the following regulatory schemes introduces information security requirements specific to the handling of credit cards?
explanation: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) introduces security requirements for the handling of credit and debit card information and transactions. The Sarbanes–Oxley Act (SOX) introduces security requirements governing the accuracy of financial statements from publicly traded companies. The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) introduces security requirements for the handling of protected health information. The Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA) introduces security requirements for the handling of student educational records.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.042
question_text: Brad works for a government agency that uses a fiscal year beginning on October 1. He is planning for an expense that will occur on November 15, 2024. During what calendar and fiscal years will this expense take place?
explanation: Brad’s organization uses a fiscal year beginning on October 1. This means that the fiscal year will be one year ahead of the calendar year during the months July through December. November 2024 is a month during calendar year 2024 and, because it is after the fiscal year changes, fiscal year 2025.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.043
question_text: Gavin is creating a report to management on the results of his most recent risk assessment. In his report, he would like to identify the remaining level of risk to the organization after adopting security controls. What term best describes this current level of risk?
explanation: The residual risk is the level of risk that remains after controls have been applied to mitigate risks. Inherent risk is the original risk that existed prior to the controls. Control risk is new risk introduced by the addition of controls to the environment. Mitigated risk is the risk that has been addressed by existing controls.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.044
question_text: Brandon is developing a set of risk metrics that will identify how much risk exists that may jeopardize the future security of the organization. What type of metric is he creating?
explanation: Key risk indicators (KRIs) are measures that are intended to quantify the security risk facing an organization. KRIs, unlike KPIs and KGIs, are a look forward. They attempt to show how much risk exists that may jeopardize the future security of the organization. Key performance indicators (KPIs) are metrics that demonstrate the success of the security program in achieving its objectives. KPIs are mutually agreed‐upon measures that evaluate whether a security program is meeting its defined goals. Generally speaking, KPIs are a look backwards at historical performance, providing a measuring stick to evaluate the past success of the program. Key goal indicators (KGIs) are similar to KPIs but measure progress toward defined goals. For example, if an organization has a goal to eliminate all stored social security numbers, a KGI might track the percentage of SSNs removed. KMIs are not a standard metric for cybersecurity programs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.045
question_text: Yuri is the new security manager at a mid‐sized financial institution. When he takes the job, he inherits a list of two hundred pending projects, each designed to mitigate threats to the institution. What two criteria should he use first to prioritize them?
explanation: While an organization may include control type and/or sensitivity in their prioritization process, the two most important characteristics to include in a threat response analysis are likelihood and impact.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.046
question_text: Casey is having difficulty convincing senior‐level management to invest in a data loss prevention system that she believes is essential to protecting the organization’s interests. Which one of the following approaches would best support her case?

answer_choices.label: A. Obtaining support from members of her team
B. Identifying a funding source for the system
C. Documenting clear success criteria for the implementation
D. Describing how the system supports broader organizational goals

correct_answer_label: D. Describing how the system supports broader organizational goals
explanation: While all of these options are important to the success of the initiative, the fundamental point here is that Casey is making the case to senior leadership. The most important thing she can do is to tie the system acquisition to broader organizational goals, explaining how the DLP system will enhance the organization’s ability to meet those goals.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.047
question_text: Which one of the following frameworks provides a formal process for implementing security controls that is mandatory for use in U.S. government agencies?
explanation: NIST publishes both the CSF and RMF, and it can be a little confusing to keep them straight. The RMF is a formal process for implementing security controls and authorizing system use, whereas the CSF provides a broad structure for cybersecurity controls. Compliance with the NIST RMF is mandatory for U.S. government agencies. COBIT and ISO 27001 may also provide important advice, but their use is not mandatory.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.048
question_text: Gwen is exploring a customer transaction reporting system and discovers the table shown below. What type of data minimization has most likely been used on this table?
explanation: In this case, the first 12 digits of the credit card have been removed and replaced with asterisks. This is an example of data masking.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.049
question_text: Gary is a system administrator and is testifying in court about a cybercrime incident. He brings server logs to support his testimony. What type of evidence are the server logs?
explanation: Server logs are an example of documentary evidence. Gary may ask that they be introduced in court and will then be asked to offer testimonial evidence about how he collected and preserved the evidence. This testimonial evidence authenticates the documentary evidence.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.050
question_text: Which one of the following statements is not a core principle of a governance framework?

answer_choices.label: A. A governance framework should be based on a conceptual model, identifying the key components and relationships among components, to maximize consistency and allow automation.

B. A governance framework should be open and flexible. It should allow the addition of new content and the ability to address new issue in the most flexible way, while maintaining integrity and consistency.
C. A governance framework should be developed by industry groups and then used without modification by corporations within those industries.
D. A governance framework should align to relevant major related standards, frameworks, and regulations

correct_answer_label: C. A governance framework should be developed by industry groups and then used without modification by corporations within those industries.
explanation: ISACA defines three principles for governance frameworks. They are: “A governance framework should be open and flexible. It should allow the addition of new content and the ability to address new issue in the most flexible way, while maintaining integrity and consistency. “A governance framework should be based upon a conceptual model, identifying the key components and relationships among components, to maximize consistency and allow automation. “A governance framework should align to relevant major related standards, frameworks, and regulations.” Governance frameworks may be developed by industry groups, but they may also be adapted by individual organizations to meet their specific needs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.051
question_text: Seth is an information security manager who is working with a business user to design a new business process that will require an exception to company policy. Who automatically has the authority to approve this exception?

answer_choices.label: A. Seth, as information security manager
B. The senior‐most leader in the user’s functional area

explanation: The policy approver has the implicit authority to approve exceptions to a policy unless the policy specifically removes that authority. Other managers or leaders do not automatically have the authority to grant exception to policy, although this authority may be explicitly granted to the manager by the policy or delegation from the policy approver.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.052
question_text: Harold would like to implement a security solution that allows him to correlate information from a variety of security systems and identify potential security events. Which technology best meets this need?
explanation: Security information and event management (SIEM) solutions collect information from many different security tools and correlate them to identify potential incidents. Intrusion prevention systems (IPSs) typically operate on a single source of information, such as a network tap or operating system, and do not correlate information from multiple sources. Data loss prevention (DLP) systems monitor systems and/or networks for the unauthorized exfiltration of sensitive information but do not correlate security information from multiple sources. Network access control (NAC) systems are used to determine what devices may be granted access to a network based on an organization’s security policies.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.053
question_text: What two elements go into the determination of the degree of a risk posed by an event?
explanation: The degree of risk posed by an event is determined by the probability (the likelihood that an event will occur) and the consequence (the impact of the event).

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.054
question_text: Which one of the following documents is the most critical output of a corporate governance process?
explanation: Corporate governance programs ensure that the organization sets an appropriate strategic direction, develops a plan to implement that strategy, and then executes on its strategic plan. Therefore, the strategic plan is the most important deliverable of a corporate governance process.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.055
question_text: Gwen is encrypting sensitive information for transmission to her business partner, Thomas. She is using an asymmetric encryption algorithm. What key would she use to encrypt the message to ensure its confidentiality?
explanation: When using an asymmetric encryption algorithm to achieve confidentiality, the sender of a message encrypts the message using the recipient’s public key. The recipient then decrypts the message using their own private key. As the sender of the message, Gwen should encrypt the message with the recipient (Thomas)’s public key.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.056
question_text: Which of the following cloud computing models places the most security burden on the vendor and the least security burden on the customer?
explanation: In the software‐as‐a‐service (SaaS) model, the greatest burden is placed on the vendor while the customer has little ability to modify security controls. In the platform‐as‐a‐service (PaaS) model, the customer and vendor share security responsibilities fairly evenly. The vendor has less responsibility under this approach than under SaaS. In the infrastructure‐as‐a‐service (IaaS) model, the customer bears significant responsibility for the secure configuration of computing resources. The function‐as‐a‐service (FaaS) model is a subset of the PaaS model, and the customer bears significant responsibility for the secure development of code.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.057
question_text: Which one of the following measures the actions that lead to an organization achieving its primary strategic direction?
explanation: Key performance indicators (KPIs) are metrics that show how well an organization is achieving its strategic direction. Key goal indicators (KGIs) are metrics that show how well an organization is achieving its stated goals for a given operating period. Key risk indicators (KRIs) are metrics that show an organization’s capability for effectively managing risk. KSIs are not a type of metric used in corporate governance programs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.058
question_text: Lila is the information security officer for her organization, and she is having an initial meeting with the human resources department to build a relationship between the two groups. Which one of the following HR processes is most important that Lila integrate with her security program?
explanation: The most important process for Lila should be the employee termination procedure to ensure that employee access is cut off as soon as possible after a termination action.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.059
question_text: Ben is responsible for the security of payment card information stored in a database. Policy directs that he remove the information from the database, but he cannot do this for operational reasons. He obtained an exception to policy and is seeking an appropriate compensating control to mitigate the risk. What would be his best option?
explanation: Ben should encrypt the data to provide an additional layer of protection as a compensating control. The organization has already made a policy exception, so he should not react by objecting to the exception or removing the data without authorization. Purchasing insurance may transfer some of the risk but is not a mitigating control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.060
question_text: Brian is developing the risk reporting program for his organization. Which one of the following statements is correct about risk reporting approaches?

answer_choices.label: A. Risk reporting mechanisms should provide information only when the situation changes.
B. Risk reporting mechanisms should use a strict schedule.
C. Risk reporting mechanisms should provide a method for emergency updates.
D. Risk reporting mechanisms should not specify communications intervals.

correct_answer_label: C. Risk reporting mechanisms should provide a method for emergency updates.
explanation: Information security managers should establish a regular reporting rhythm with periodic risk reports delivered at a regular frequency as well as a mechanism for rapidly providing urgent updates. In all cases, risk managers should write their reports with a strong understanding of their audience, tailoring the content to the knowledge, needs, and specific concerns of each target group.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.061
question_text: Which one of the following is not a purging activity?
explanation: Resetting a device to factory state is an example of a data clearing activity. Data purging activities include overwriting, block erase, and cryptographic erase activities when performed through the use of dedicated, standardized device commands.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.062
question_text: Paul is concerned that network devices in his organization may have exposed management interfaces. Which one of the following tests is most likely to discover this vulnerability?
explanation: Exposed management interfaces are a standard test conducted during network vulnerability scans. Application testing of any kind is unlikely to discover this type of vulnerability.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.063
question_text: Vincent is working with a contractor on the design of a new cybersecurity system. His organization has worked with this vendor in the past and has a master services agreement (MSA) in place. What type of document should Vincent prepare to describe the work that will be done in this particular engagement?
explanation: Because a master services agreement is already in place, Vincent can simply write a statement of work (SOW) describing this project and referencing the existing MSA. It is not appropriate subject matter for a nondisclosure agreement (NDA) that focuses on confidentiality or a less formal vehicle, such as a memorandum of understanding (MOU). It is also not necessary to create a full contract, since the MSA is already in place providing formal contract language.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.064
question_text: Katie is helping her organization enter into a new consulting arrangement with a cybersecurity firm that will provide them with assistance on a series of upcoming projects. She would like to create an umbrella agreement that provides the general terms and then create engagement‐specific documents that detail the deliverables for each project under that umbrella agreement. What term best describes the umbrella agreement?
explanation: Master service agreements (MSA) provide an umbrella contract for the work that a vendor does with an organization over an extended period of time. The MSA typically includes detailed security and privacy requirements. Each time the organization begins a new project with the vendor, they may then create a statement of work (SOW) that contains project‐specific details and references the MSA. A memorandum of understanding (MOU) is a letter written to document aspects of the relationship. MOUs are an informal mechanism that allows the parties to document their relationship to avoid future misunderstandings. Business partnership agreements (BPAs) exist when two organizations agree to do business with each other in a partnership. For example, if two companies jointly develop and market a product, the BPA might specify each partner’s responsibilities and the division of profits.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.065
question_text: Which one of the following statements about project management is correct?

answer_choices.label: A. Project managers should conduct an initial risk assessment and may then use that assessment for the duration of the project.
B. Project management rarely results in changes to business practices.
C. Project management procedures should incorporate ongoing risk assessments.
D. Risk management should be treated as a one‐time project.

correct_answer_label: C. Project management procedures should incorporate ongoing risk assessments.
explanation: Project management procedures should incorporate risk assessments that identify new or changed risks that arise during the course of a project and address them appropriately. Mature organizations don’t treat risk management efforts as siloed projects but rather as an integral component of their day‐to‐day work.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.066
question_text: What data minimization technique replaces personal identifiers with unique identifiers that may be cross‐referenced with a lookup table?
explanation: Tokenization replaces personal identifiers that might directly reveal an individual’s identity with a unique identifier using a lookup table. Hashing uses a cryptographic hash function to replace sensitive identifiers with an irreversible alternative identifier. Salting these values with a random number prior to hashing them makes these hashed values resistant to a type of attack known as a rainbow table attack.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.067
question_text: Which one of the phases of incident response involves primarily active undertakings designed to limit the damage that an attacker might cause?
explanation: The containment, eradication, and recovery phase of incident response includes active undertakings designed to minimize the damage caused by the incident and restore normal operations as quickly as possible.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.068
question_text: Brian is reviewing the integration of his organization’s existing business processes with the organization’s enterprise risk management program. Which one of the following processes is least likely to involve activities that may trigger new risk assessments?
explanation: The software/systems development, procurement, and project management functions are all involved in creating and modifying systems and processes. Therefore, they are very likely to trigger the need for a risk assessment. The order processing function typically conducts routine work that does not change business activity and would be less likely to generate the need for a risk assessment.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.069
question_text: What type of security awareness exercise always provides a real‐world assessment of the likelihood that users will fall victim to social engineering attacks?
explanation: While any security awareness effort may include real‐world assessments, phishing simulations are a direct implementation of this idea. They send fake phishing messages to users and those who click on the message are sent to a training program to help them better detect fraudulent messages. The simulation also allows the organization to measure how well prepared its users are to defend themselves against phishing attacks.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.070
question_text: Bill is designing an information security governance strategy for his organization. His primary objective is to choose a strategy that minimizes the variability in control implementation across the organization. Which governance strategy would best meet this goal?
explanation: Centralized approaches to security governance provide a single authority for information security activities and minimize variation. Distributed approaches to information security governance allow shared responsibility across business units and offer flexibility, which results in some variability. A hybrid governance approach combines elements of centralized and distributed models and does not provide as much consistency as a pure centralized approach. Flexibility may be a desired characteristic of some governance models, but it is not a governance strategy in and of itself.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.071
question_text: Jane is trying to determine the appropriate level of security controls for a resource. What factor should she weight most heavily in her analysis?
explanation: The primary factor used in determining the appropriate level of security control for an asset is the asset’s classification. The threat environment, replacement cost, and control cost may be considered as well, but Jane should focus primarily on selecting controls appropriate to the asset classification.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.072
question_text: What is the primary purpose of an incident management program?

answer_choices.label: A. To alert essential individuals when an incident occurs
B. To identify and assess incidents and prevent their recurrence
C. To designate an individual responsible for information security
D. To conduct lessons learned sessions after incidents

correct_answer_label: B. To identify and assess incidents and prevent their recurrence
explanation: The goal of an incident response program is to identify and assess incidents that occur and use that information to prevent future incidents. While many incident management programs do include lessons learned sessions, notification procedures, and responsibility designations, these are not their primary purpose.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.073
question_text: Ann is designing a transaction processing system and would like to implement a control that can ensure the authenticity of orders received from customers. Which one of the following controls would best meet her goal?
explanation: Digital signatures are used to achieve the goal of nonrepudiation and may be used to verify the authenticity of an order. Encryption may be used to ensure the confidentiality of transmissions, but it does not, by itself, provide a means of verifying the authenticity of the transaction. Hashing may be used as a component of verifying authenticity through a digital signature process, but it is not sufficient to confirm the authenticity of an order. Digital certificates may be used to share the public keys used to verify digital signatures when checking the authenticity of an order, but they are not sufficient to verify an order’s authenticity by themselves.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.074
question_text: What is the major disadvantage to running intrusive vulnerability scans?

answer_choices.label: A. Intrusive scans may disrupt production systems.
B. Intrusive scans may jeopardize personal privacy.
C. Intrusive scans require expensive scanning tools.
D. Intrusive scans require manual configuration.

correct_answer_label: A. Intrusive scans may disrupt production systems.
explanation: Some plug‐ins perform tests that may actually disrupt activity on a production system or, in the worst case, damage content on those systems. These intrusive plug‐ins are a tricky situation. Administrators want to run these scans because they may identify problems that could be exploited by a malicious source. At the same time, cybersecurity professionals clearly don’t want to cause problems on the organization’s network, and as a result, they may limit their scans to nonintrusive plug‐ins. Intrusive scans do not pose any risk to personal privacy, and they do not require any tools or configuration not required for nonintrusive scans.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.075
question_text: Chris is concerned about the possibility that former employees will disclose sensitive personal information about customers to unauthorized individuals. What is the best mechanism that Chris can use to manage this risk?
explanation: All of the mechanisms listed here may be used to protect private information. However, acceptable use policies, privacy policies, and data ownership policies are internal policies that would not be binding on former employees. To manage this risk, Chris’s organization should have all employees sign nondisclosure agreements (NDAs) that remain binding after the end of the employment relationship.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.076
question_text: Acme Widgets is a publicly traded corporation that recently experienced a major security breach. As a result, the company is considering disciplinary action against senior executives. Who would be the appropriate group to consider action against the chief executive officer (CEO), including termination of that individual?
explanation: In a publicly traded corporation, the board of directors exercises oversight of the CEO. The directors are elected by the shareholders to represent their interests, as the shareholders are not able to exercise direct operational control of the organization. Government regulators may take action against the firm but would generally not be involved in any decision regarding termination of the CEO. Other senior leaders may consider actions against other employees but would not have the authority to discipline the CEO.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.077
question_text: What ISO standard provides guidance on risk management practices?
explanation: The International Organization for Standardization (ISO) publishes ISO 31000, covering risk management. ISO 27001 and 27002 cover cybersecurity, and ISO 27701 covers privacy controls.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.078
question_text: Which one of the following individuals is normally responsible for fulfilling the operational data protection responsibilities delegated by senior management, such as validating data integrity, testing backups, and managing security policies?
explanation: The data custodian role is assigned to an individual who is responsible for implementing the security controls defined by policy and senior management. The data owner does bear ultimate responsibility for these tasks, but the data owner is typically a senior leader who delegates operational responsibility to a data custodian.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.079
question_text: Carl’s organization is subject to PCI DSS. He determines that he will be unable to meet one of the PCI DSS objectives due to technical limitations and has obtained permission from his merchant bank to implement an alternative mechanism in place of the PCI DSS requirement. What type of control is Carl implementing?
explanation: The scenario does not tell us whether the control is preventive, detective, or corrective. We do know, however, that it is being used in place of another control requirement that Carl’s organization is unable to meet and is, therefore, a compensating control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.080
question_text: Tina recently completed a risk management review and identified that the organization is susceptible to a man‐in‐the‐middle attack. After review with her manager, they jointly decided that accepting the risk is the most appropriate strategy. What should Tina do next?

answer_choices.label: A. Repeat the business impact assessment.

explanation: After accepting a risk, the organization takes no action other than to document the risk as accepted. Implementing additional security controls or designing a remediation plan would not be risk acceptance but would instead fit into the category of risk mitigation. There is no need to repeat the business impact assessment.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.081
question_text: Tony purchases virtual machines from Microsoft Azure and acquires them exclusively for use by his organization. What model of cloud computing is this?
explanation: This is an example of public cloud computing because Tony is using a public cloud provider, Microsoft Azure. The fact that Tony is limiting access to virtual machines to his own organization is not relevant because the determining factor for the cloud model is whether the underlying infrastructure is shared, not whether virtualized resources are shared.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.082
question_text: Terri is reviewing her organization’s cybersecurity program and conducting external research. During this work, she discovers that there is a new hacking group with advanced capabilities targeting organizations in her industry. How should she classify this group when she performs a SWOT analysis?
explanation: Terri should realize that a hacking group is an external force, rather than an internal force, so it should be classified as either an opportunity or a threat. Additionally, the presence of this hacking group would have a potentially negative impact on the organization, so it is either a threat or a weakness. Combining these two facts identifies that the hacking group is a threat and should be placed into the threat quadrant of the SWOT analysis.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.083
question_text: Alice is responding to a cybersecurity incident and notices a system that she suspects is compromised. She places this system on a quarantine VLAN with limited access to other networked systems. What containment strategy is Alice pursuing?
explanation: In a segmentation approach, the suspect system is placed on a separate network where it has very limited access to other networked resources.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.084
question_text: Jen is conducting a penetration test against a nonprofit organization where she volunteers. She didn’t want to bother their small staff with the details of the test but plans to share any findings she has with leadership to help them improve their security. What term best describes this test?
explanation: You might be tempted to answer “white hat” to this question because Jen has good intentions, but that is not sufficient to meet the criteria for a white‐hat test. Jen does not have explicit permission to perform the test, so it is not a white‐hat exercise. Instead, it is a gray‐hat exercise, a test executed without permission but for benevolent purposes. A black‐hat exercise has malicious intent, and blue‐hat exercises do not exist.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.085
question_text: What term describes an organization’s willingness to tolerate risk within its environment?
explanation: An organization’s risk appetite is its willingness to tolerate risk within the environment. If an organization is extremely risk averse, it may choose to conduct scans more frequently to minimize the amount of time between when a vulnerability comes into existence and when it is detected by a scan.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.086
question_text: Which one of the following requirements is the most important for an access control system?

answer_choices.label: B. Integrating with security logging systems

explanation: The core requirement of any access control system is that it must prevent unauthorized access. Single sign‐on technology is a major convenience for users but is less important than meeting the basic requirement of preventing unauthorized access. Two‐factor authentication is an important security control for high‐risk assets, but it is less important than meeting the basic requirement of preventing unauthorized access. Integration with security logging systems is an important requirement for access control systems, but it is less important than meeting the basic requirement of preventing unauthorized access.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.087
question_text: Don created a budget for his information security program and obtained funding from leadership. During the year, expenses fluctuate so that they do not directly align with the budget. When Don reports his final financial results, which one of the following situations would be the best for Don’s organization?

answer_choices.label: A. The program is significantly over budget.
B. The program is slightly over budget.
C. The program is slightly under budget.
D. The program is significantly under budget.

correct_answer_label: C. The program is slightly under budget.
explanation: The best‐case scenario is that expenses are close to the budgeted amount but do not exceed the actual budget. Budget overages are difficult because funds may not be available to cover all expenses. When expenses are significantly under budget, the organization suffers an opportunity cost because those funds could have been used for other purposes or returned to shareholders as profit.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.088
question_text: Ben wants to provide predictive information about his organization’s risk exposure in an automated way as part of an ongoing organizational risk management plan. What should he use to do this?
explanation: Key risk indicators (KRIs) are often used to monitor risk for organizations that establish an ongoing risk management program. Using automated data gathering and tools that allow data to be digested and summarized can provide predictive information about how organizational risks are changing. KPIs are key performance indicators, which are used to assess how an organization is performing. Quantitative risk assessments are good for point‐in‐time views with detailed valuation and measurement‐based risk assessments, whereas a penetration test would provide details of how well an organization’s security controls are working.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.089
question_text: Juan is a forensic investigator collecting evidence that may be used in an upcoming criminal trial. Which one of the following concerns should be Juan’s highest priority?

answer_choices.label: A. Preserving the chain of custody
D. Using write blockers to collect evidence

correct_answer_label: A. Preserving the chain of custody
explanation: The chain of custody requires that Juan be able to clearly document every change in control of the evidence from the time it is collected until it is presented at trial. Evidence may be thrown out if there is any question about the chain of custody. While Juan may be asked about his expertise at trial, it is more important to preserve the chain of custody for the evidence. While some investigators may choose to film their collection of evidence, this is not a requirement and is not as important as preserving the chain of custody. Although many forensic investigators do use write‐blockers as a safety mechanism while collecting evidence, their use is not a requirement and is not as important as preserving the chain of custody.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.090
question_text: Gary discovers that his organization is storing some old files in a cloud service that are exposed to the world. He deletes those files. What type of risk management strategy is this?
explanation: Gary is changing business practices to eliminate the risk entirely. This is, therefore, an example of risk avoidance.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.091
question_text: What type of application testing analyzes code while a tester manipulates inputs to the application?
explanation: Interactive testing combines static and dynamic analysis, performing static code analysis while the attacker interacts with application inputs. Static analysis analyzes code but does not manipulate inputs. Dynamic analysis manipulates inputs without performing code analysis. Footprinting is a network reconnaissance technique and not an application testing technique.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.092
question_text: Helen designed a new payroll system that she offers to her customers. She hosts the payroll system in AWS and her customers access it through the web. What tier of cloud computing best describes Helen’s service?
explanation: Helen is using IaaS services to create her payroll product. She is then offering that payroll service to her customers as an SaaS solution.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.093
question_text: David is an IT professional responsible for applying, monitoring, and maintaining access controls to a filesystem containing sensitive information used by the human resources department. He works closely with the management of that department to identify appropriate permissions. What term best describes David’s role in relation to this data?
explanation: Data custodians, or data processors, are the individuals who actually store and process sensitive information. As an IT professional implementing the directions of the data owners and stewards in the HR department, David is a data custodian. Data subjects are the individuals referred to in these records. Technically, David may also be a data subject because he is likely an employee of the organization and would be in the HR database. However, the question is asking for the role that best describes David, which is his primary role as the data custodian for the entire dataset rather than the incidental fact that he is one of many data subjects described in the records.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.094
question_text: Belinda is attempting to determine the RPO for a database server that she administers. Which piece of information will best help her determine this value?

answer_choices.label: A. The amount of time required to recover a service.
B. The cost to recover a service
C. The amount of time that a service may be down
D. The amount of data loss that the organization considers acceptable

correct_answer_label: D. The amount of data loss that the organization considers acceptable
explanation: The recovery point objective (RPO) is based on the amount of data loss that the organization considers acceptable in the event of a disaster, not the amount of time that a service may be down, the amount of time required to recover a service, or the cost to recover a service.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.095
question_text: Which one of the following is not a common use of formal incident reports?
explanation: There are many potential uses for written incident reports. First, it creates an institutional memory of the incident that is useful when developing new security controls and training new security team members. Second, it may serve as an important record of the incident if there is ever legal action that results from the incident. These reports should be classified and not disclosed to external parties.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.096
question_text: Bev is developing a risk management strategy for the effects of a cybersecurity incident on her organization. She chooses to purchase an insurance policy to cover the financial risks. What risk management strategy is Bev pursuing?
explanation: Purchasing an insurance policy transfers some of the risk to a third party, the insurance company. This is an example of a risk transference approach. Risk mitigation actions reduce the likelihood and/or impact of a risk if it occurs. Purchasing insurance does not reduce either the likelihood or impact, but it does transfer some of the risk to a third party. Risk acceptance involves a business decision to take no action in response to a risk. Purchasing insurance is an action, so this is not an example of risk acceptance. Risk avoidance alters business operations to eliminate a risk. There is no indication that Bev is changing any business operations, so this is not an example of risk avoidance.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.097
question_text: Ruth is reviewing her organization’s business continuity and disaster recovery plans. In it, she comes across the following statement: “In the event that the payroll system fails, the IT team must restore access within 48 hours to avoid unacceptable levels of damage to the business.” What type of statement is this?
explanation: The recovery time objective (RTO) is the amount of time that may elapse between when a system fails and when it is recovered before causing substantial harm to the business. The recovery point objective (RPO) is the amount of acceptable data loss in the event of a disaster, expressed as the period of time from which data may be lost. The service level agreement (SLA) is an agreement between a vendor and a customer regarding technology services. Mail transfer agents (MTAs) are a component of email infrastructure and are not related to disaster recovery.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.098
question_text: Which one of the following tools may be used to isolate an attacker so that they may not cause damage to production systems but may still be observed by cybersecurity analysts?
explanation: Sandboxes are isolation tools used to contain attackers within an environment where they believe they are conducting an attack but, in reality, are operating in a benign environment.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.099
question_text: Betty is preparing to publish a new information security policy for her organization and is drafting an email message announcing the new policy and informing employees about their responsibilities. Who would be the most effective signatory for the message?
explanation: The president or CEO is the senior‐most person within an organization and has both name recognition and authority that lends credibility to the message. While the CISO bears ultimate responsibility for security, it is likely that most of the organization does not know them, making the CISO a less desirable choice for signing the message. The CIO is a senior executive who might sign the message, but it would be more effective if it originated from the president/CEO. Policy administrators are not well known throughout an organization and typically have very little authority, making them a poor choice for sending out the announcement.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.100
question_text: What phase of the Electronic Discovery Reference Model examines information to remove information subject to attorney‐client privilege?
explanation: Review examines the information resulting from the Processing phase to determine what information is responsive to the request and remove any information protected by attorney‐client privilege. Identification locates the information that may be responsive to a discovery request when the organization believes that litigation is likely. Collection gathers the relevant information centrally for use in the eDiscovery process. Processing screens the collected information to perform a “rough cut” of irrelevant information, reducing the amount of information requiring detailed screening.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.101
question_text: Alan is concerned about the risk posed by attackers exploiting flaws in his organization’s web applications to gain access to the underlying database through a SQL injection attack. What would be the most effective control in this scenario?
explanation: Input validation limits the types of information that users may submit to the web application and can strip out SQL injection attacks before they are executed. Web server patches are an important security control, but they will affect the application’s access to the database. Network firewalls are an important security control, but they do not perform application filtering and would not be effective against a SQL injection attack. Host firewalls are important security controls, but they cannot identify SQL injection attacks.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.102
question_text: Lou mounted the sign below on the fence surrounding his organization’s datacenter. What control type best describes this control?
explanation: This question is a little tricky. The use of an actual guard dog could be considered a deterrent, physical, or detective control. It could even be a compensating control in some circumstances. However, the question asks about the presence of a sign and does not state that an actual dog is used. The sign only has value as a deterrent control. Be careful when facing exam questions like this to read the details of the question.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.103
question_text: Kevin is attempting to determine whether he can destroy a cache of old records that he discovered. What type of policy would most directly answer his question?
explanation: The most relevant policy here is the organization’s data retention policy, which should outline the standards for keeping records before destruction or disposal.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.104
question_text: What step of the Electronic Discovery Reference Model ensures that information that may be subject to discovery is not altered?
explanation: Preservation ensures that potentially discoverable information is protected against alteration or deletion. Production places the information into a format that may be shared with others and delivers it to other parties, such as opposing counsel. Processing screens the collected information to perform a “rough cut” of irrelevant information, reducing the amount of information requiring detailed screening. Presentation displays the information to witnesses, the court, and other parties.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.105
question_text: Sean is developing a new information security policy for his organization. Which of the following statements is correct about the type of input he should solicit?

answer_choices.label: A. Sean should gather input from all employees of his organization.
B. Sean should only gather input from members of his own team.
C. Sean only needs to gather input from senior leaders.
D. Sean should gather input from all affected stakeholders

correct_answer_label: D. Sean should gather input from all affected stakeholders
explanation: Sean should obtain input from a broad group of people, but only from relevant stakeholders. He doesn’t necessarily need to follow the advice he receives from everyone, but everyone who will be a stakeholder should feel that their input was solicited and heard during the process.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.106
question_text: Elise is helping her organization prepare to evaluate and adopt a new cloud‐based human resource management (HRM) system vendor. What would be the most appropriate minimum security standard for her to require of possible vendors?

answer_choices.label: A. Compliance with all laws and regulations
B. Handling information in the same manner the organization would
C. Compliance with the vendor’s own policies
D. Elimination of all identified security risks

correct_answer_label: B. Handling information in the same manner the organization would
explanation: The most appropriate standard to use as a baseline when evaluating vendors is to determine whether the vendor’s security controls meet the organization’s own standards. Compliance with laws and regulations should be included in that requirement and are a necessary, but not sufficient, condition for working with the vendor. Vendor compliance with their own policies also fits into the category of necessary, but not sufficient, controls, as the vendor’s policy may be weaker than the organization’s own requirements. The elimination of all identified security risks is an impossible requirement for a potential vendor to meet.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.107
question_text: Linda is conducting a SWOT analysis of her organization’s information security program. She discovers that the organization does not have appropriate procedures in place to monitor and update firewall configurations. How should she classify this situation in her analysis?
explanation: The firewall is a control operated by the organization, so this is an internal issue and would thus be classified as a strength or weakness. Opportunities and threats are external forces. The lack of configuration controls is a negative factor, so it is a weakness, rather than a strength.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.108
question_text: Jen is conducting a penetration test for a client. The client did not provide her with any details about their systems in advance of the test and Jen is determining this information using reconnaissance techniques. What type of test is Jen performing?
explanation: Black‐box tests are intended to replicate what an attacker would encounter. Testers are not provided with access to or information about an environment, and instead, they must gather information, discover vulnerabilities, and make their way through an infrastructure or systems like an attacker would. White‐box tests are performed with full knowledge of the underlying technology, configurations, and settings that make up the target. Gray‐box tests are a blend of black‐box and white‐box testing. Blue‐box tests are not a type of penetration test.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.109
question_text: Morgan is configuring a new alarm system to protect her organization’s data center from physical intrusions. What type of control is she configuring?
explanation: This is an example of a detective control—one that identifies attacks that are in progress, such as a physical intrusion. Preventive controls seek to stop an attack from occurring in the first place. Corrective controls attempt to restore normal operations after an incident takes place. Deterrent controls seek to prevent attackers from attempting an attack.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.110
question_text: Gary is collecting evidence from the hard drive of a system that was involved in a security incident. What technology may Gary use to later demonstrate that the evidence was not tampered with after collection?
explanation: Hashes create a digital fingerprint of the evidence that may later be compared to the current version to demonstrate that no changes were made. Write blockers will prevent the alteration of evidence but do not provide a means to demonstrate to others that the evidence was not altered. Digital certificates are used to communicate public encryption keys and would not be useful in this scenario. Backups may serve as a source of evidence, but they do not provide the evidence that Gary needs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.111
question_text: Ben is responding to a security incident and determines that the attacker is using systems on Ben’s network to attack a third party. Which one of the following containment approaches will prevent Ben’s systems from being used in this manner?
explanation: Only removal of the compromised system from the network will stop the attack against other systems. Isolated and/or segmented systems are still permitted access to the Internet and could continue their attack. Detection is a purely passive activity that does not disrupt the attacker at all.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.112
question_text: Harold is the chief information security officer for his company. He is working on the resource requirements for his efforts. Where should those resource requirements be listed?
explanation: The security strategy should include detailed resource requirements needed to implement the security strategy. Security policies should contain guiding principles for the security program and would not contain resource requirements. Security procedures should contain step‐by‐step instructions for security processes and would not include resource requirements. The security team roster would contain a listing of employees and is not the appropriate location for resource requirements.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.113
question_text: Kira would like to implement a security control that can implement access restrictions across all of the SaaS solutions used by her organization. What control would best meet her needs?
explanation: Cloud access security brokers (CASBs) are designed specifically for this situation: enforcing security controls across cloud providers. A secure web gateway (SWG) may be able to achieve Kira’s goal, but it would be more difficult to do so. Security groups and resource policies are controls used in IaaS environments.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.114
question_text: Brooke is a cybersecurity professional for a financial services firm. In her role, she is responsible for building and maintaining an inventory of the sensitive information used by the firm. Which one of the following categories of information is she least likely to encounter?
explanation: Financial firms are not likely to encounter protected health information (PHI), which are the HIPAA‐regulated medical records maintained by health‐care providers and other HIPAA‐covered entities. They are quite likely to have personally identifiable information (PII), payment card information (PCI), and other customer‐related information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.115
question_text: Phil is investigating a security incident where the attackers shut down his organization’s database server. They do not appear to have actually gained access to the system, but they have shut it down using some type of remote exploit. What principle of cybersecurity was most directly violated during this incident?
explanation: This is an example of an availability breach. By shutting down the database server, the attackers were able to deny authorized users access to the server.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.116
question_text: Seth works in an organization that uses a rigid hierarchical management structure. He frequently encounters the CIO in the hallway and engages her in conversation and considers her a friendly acquaintance. The CIO is Seth’s third‐level supervisor. Seth has an idea for a new cybersecurity initiative. What would be the best way for Seth to gain approval for his idea?

answer_choices.label: A. Schedule a meeting with the CIO, his direct supervisor, and the manager who sits between them to propose the idea
B. Informally discuss the idea with the CIO during a chance encounter
C. Schedule a meeting with the CIO and his direct supervisor to propose the idea
D. Informally discuss the idea with his direct supervisor.

correct_answer_label: D. Informally discuss the idea with his direct supervisor.
explanation: In a hierarchical organization, Seth should follow the chain‐of‐command. In this case, that means he should bring the idea to his direct supervisor before engaging anyone else in the organization, whether formally or informally.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.117
question_text: Which one of the following software development models focuses on the early and continuous delivery of software?
explanation: One of the core principles of the Agile approach to software development is to ensure customer satisfaction via early and continuous delivery of software.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.118
question_text: Rob is the CISO of a mid‐sized company and he is working on developing a strategic plan for the organization’s cybersecurity function. He will need a commitment of funding and staff time to complete his plan. What is the most important component of obtaining the resources he needs?

answer_choices.label: A. Planning ahead for staff assignments
B. Making clear requests for funding
C. Obtaining ongoing commitment from senior leaders
D. Communicating a clear timetable for initiatives

correct_answer_label: C. Obtaining ongoing commitment from senior leaders
explanation: All of these items are important to Rob’s success, but we are asked to identify the most important component in this question. That would be obtaining ongoing commitment from senior leadership for his initiative. Without high‐level support, Rob will not be able to obtain the resources that he needs.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.119
question_text: Which of the following is not an external factor that would normally prompt a review of the organization’s risk assessment?
explanation: All of these factors may prompt a risk reassessment to ensure that changes to existing, or new, risk scenarios are identified and managed appropriately. However, a system configuration change is an internal factor, not an external factor. Changes in the geopolitical climate, threat landscape, or the regulatory regime are external factors.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.120
question_text: Jody is reviewing the results of a business impact assessment and attempting to determine which risk she should address first. Which one of the following should she select as her highest priority?

answer_choices.label: A. The risk with the highest ALE
B. The risk with the lowest ALE
C. The risk with the highest SLE
D. The risk with the lowest SLE

correct_answer_label: A. The risk with the highest ALE
explanation: Jody should address the risk that is expected to cause the most damage to the organization. She can do this by identifying the risk with the highest annualized loss expectancy (ALE). The ALE is a measure of both likelihood and impact, describing the total damage expected in any given year.
