The single loss expectancy (SLE) is a measure of impact only, as it does not incorporate likelihood information.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.121
question_text: Who has primary responsibility for the governance of a publicly traded corporation?
explanation: Shareholders do own the organization, but they delegate governance of the organization to a board of directors that they elect. This board has primary responsibility for corporate governance. The president or CEO may be the most senior executive within an organization, but they are also subject to the governance of the board of directors.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.122
question_text: Which one of the following pieces of information is most critical to conducting a solid incident recovery effort?

answer_choices.label: D. Root cause of the attack

correct_answer_label: D. Root cause of the attack
explanation: Understanding the root cause of an attack is critical to the incident recovery effort. Analysts should examine all available information to help reconstruct the attacker’s actions. This information is crucial to remediating security controls and preventing future similar attacks.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.123
question_text: Karl is developing an information security strategy for his organization. Which one of the following elements should be the most important consideration?
explanation: All of the factors described here may affect an organization’s security strategy, but the highest priority of a strategy document is to align the security function with the business. Therefore, Karl should make aligning the document with business goals his highest priority. Strategy documents would generally not contain detailed information about technology solutions, threat analyses, or encryption key management.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.124
question_text: Shelly is writing a document that describes the steps that incident response teams will follow upon first notice of a potential incident. What type of document is she creating?
explanation: Procedures provide checklist‐style sets of step‐by‐step instructions guiding how employees should react in a given circumstance. Procedures commonly guide the early stages of incident response

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.125
question_text: Gary is designing a security program and wants to reduce the risk that employees will share sensitive information accidentally via social media. What would be the most effective control against this risk?

answer_choices.label: D. Blocking social media at the firewall

explanation: Employee use of social media requires tact and discretion. Employees are likely to access social media despite any technical controls put in place, so the most effective way to protect against the loss of sensitive information is education and awareness. Firewalls may block access to social media in some cases, but employees will likely continue to access social media on their personal devices. Data loss prevention technology may identify some unintended exposure of sensitive information, but employees are likely to access social media from their personal mobile devices or from home, where this control would not apply. Content filtering may block access to social media in some cases, but employees will likely continue to access social media on their personal devices.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.126
question_text: Tim is using the NIST Cybersecurity Framework to perform a risk assessment of his organization’s current cybersecurity posture. As he reviews the state of affairs, he discovers that his organization has a risk management policy and formally approved practices but does not conduct lessons learned sessions. What tier best describes the organization’s status?
explanation: The adoption of formal risk management policies is a hallmark of Tier 3: Repeatable under the NIST Cybersecurity Framework. Moving to the top tier, Tier 4: Adaptive, would require that the organization adapts its cybersecurity practices based on previous and current cybersecurity activities, including lessons learned and predictive indicators. Because the organization does not yet conduct lessons learned sessions, they have not reached this tier.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.127
question_text: Which statement best describes the purpose of an information security strategy?

answer_choices.label: A. To provide senior leaders with the assurance that information security controls function effectively
B. To measure the effectiveness of the information security organization
C. To create a plan for moving from the current state of information security to a future desired state
D. To identify weaknesses in the organization’s security posture that require remediation

correct_answer_label: C. To create a plan for moving from the current state of information security to a future desired state
explanation: The information security strategy begins with an assessment of the current state of the organization and a comparison to the desired state of security based on the organization’s control objectives. It then outlines a plan for working from that current state to achieve the desired state through clearly articulated goals.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.128
question_text: What phase of the vendor management process should include establishing procedures for security incident notification?
explanation: Security incident procedures should be established prior to beginning the operational phase of the relationship. Therefore, they should be created during the vendor onboarding process, rather than during any of the other phases that come after the vendor is already up and running.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.129
question_text: Which one of the following activities is not normally conducted during the recovery validation phase?

answer_choices.label: B. Verify the permissions assigned to each account
D. Verify logging is functioning properly

explanation: New firewall rules, if required, would be implemented during the eradication and recovery phase. The validation phase includes verifying accounts and permissions, verifying that logging is working properly, and conducting vulnerability scans.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.130
question_text: Dave is preparing a document for users that helps them encrypt their hard drives to protect sensitive information and comply with corporate policy. It includes step‐by‐step instructions. What type of document is he creating?
explanation: Procedures contain step‐by‐step instructions for completing a security‐related activity. Policies contain high‐level statements of security objectives. They do not contain step‐by‐step guidance. Standards provide the detailed configuration parameters necessary to implement security policy. They do not normally contain implementation instructions. Guidelines provide best practices for users to follow but they are not mandatory and do not normally include step‐by‐step instructions.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.131
question_text: Which one of the following is not typically found in a cybersecurity incident report?
explanation: Incident reports should include a chronology of events, estimates of the impact, and documentation of lessons learned, in addition to other information. Incident response efforts should not normally focus on uncovering the identity of the attacker, so this information would not be found in an incident report.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.132
question_text: Cindy is performing a risk assessment of her organization and would like to use an industry standard that will help her determine her current security posture for each of the major cybersecurity functions, describe a target state for those controls, and then develop a plan for achieving that target state. What framework is best suited for this task?
explanation: NIST publishes both a Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) and a Risk Management Framework (RMF). The RMF is a formal process for implementing security controls and authorizing system use, whereas the CSF provides a broad structure for cybersecurity controls. The CSF is intended to help organizations describe their current cybersecurity posture, describe their target state for cybersecurity, identify and prioritize opportunities for improvement within the context of a continuous and repeatable process, assess progress toward the target state, and communicate among internal and external stakeholders about cybersecurity risk. ISO 27701 contains standard guidance for managing privacy controls. ISO 31000 provides guidelines for risk management programs. This document is not specific to cybersecurity or privacy but covers risk management in a general way so that it may be applied to any risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.133
question_text: Sondra determines that an attacker has gained access to a server containing critical business files and wishes to ensure that the attacker cannot delete those files. Which one of the following strategies would meet Sondra’s goal?
explanation: Even removing a system from the network doesn’t guarantee that the attack will not continue. In the example given in Chapter 8, an attacker can run a script on the server that detects when it has been removed from the network and then proceeds to destroy data stored on the server.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.134
question_text: During an operational investigation, what type of analysis might an organization undertake to prevent similar incidents in the future?
explanation: Root cause analysis seeks to identify the reason that an operational issue occurred. The root cause analysis often highlights issues that require remediation to prevent similar incidents in the future. Forensic analysis is used to obtain evidence from digital systems. Network traffic analysis is an example of a forensic analysis category. Fagan inspection is a software testing technique.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.135
question_text: Ron is the CISO of a U.S. company that is entering into a business partnership with a European firm. The European firm will be sending his company customer records to run through Ron’s firm’s proprietary credit scoring algorithm. What role will Ron’s company have relative to the customer data?
explanation: Ron’s company is a data processor in this instance, as it is receiving records from the European firm. The European firm is the data controller in this case, as they bear responsibility for the data. The individuals described in the records are the data subjects. Data owners are tasked with making decisions about data such as who receives access to it and how it is used.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.136
question_text: Xavier is a data custodian responsible for maintaining databases with sensitive information, including Social Security numbers. He would like to protect those SSNs from prying eyes but will need to be able to retrieve the original value on occasion. What data protection technique would be most appropriate for his use?
explanation: Tokenization replaces sensitive values in a table with a non‐sensitive token value. Employees with access to the lookup table may use these tokens to retrieve the original value when necessary. Masking, redaction, and hashing are all techniques that destroy the original value, making it impractical to retrieve it.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.137
question_text: Which one of the following U.S. government classification levels requires the highest degree of security control?
explanation: Top Secret is the highest level of classification under the U.S. system and, therefore, requires the highest level of security control.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.138
question_text: Robin is planning to conduct a risk assessment in her organization. She is concerned that it will be difficult to perform the assessment because she needs to include information about both tangible and intangible assets. What would be the most effective risk assessment strategy for her to use?

answer_choices.label: B. Combination of quantitative and qualitative risk assessment
D. Neither quantitative nor qualitative risk assessment

correct_answer_label: B. Combination of quantitative and qualitative risk assessment
explanation: Robin would have achieved the best results by combining elements of quantitative and qualitative risk assessment. Quantitative risk assessment excels at analyzing tangible, financial risks, whereas qualitative risk assessment is good for intangible risks. Combining the two techniques provides a well‐rounded risk picture.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.139
question_text: Jen is helping create a new cybersecurity policy for her organization. Her supervisor agrees with the policy approach but there is an influential engineer on her team who she thinks will disagree. What should Jen do next?

answer_choices.label: A. Ask her manager to bring the policy to senior leadership for approval
B. Ask her manager to approve the policy
C. Discuss the policy with the engineer and try to build consensus
D. Revise the policy before submitting it

correct_answer_label: C. Discuss the policy with the engineer and try to build consensus
explanation: Jen should certainly respect the formal chain of command within her organization, but it is also important to acknowledge informal power and influence. If possible, she should try to help the influential engineer become a supporter before moving forward.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.140
question_text: Seth is evaluating a new source of potential threat intelligence. Which one of the following questions would be least important to his evaluation?
explanation: Let’s eliminate the most important questions that a threat researcher can ask. First, it is important to know whether the information is timely or not. A feed that is operating on delay can cause you to miss a threat, or to react after the threat is no longer relevant. Second, you need to know whether the information is accurate. You must know whether you can you rely on what it says, and how likely is it that the assessment is valid. Finally, you must know whether the information is relevant to your organization. If it describes the wrong platform, software, or reason for the organization to be targeted, the data may be very timely, very accurate, and completely irrelevant to your organization. It is less important to know whether the information is proprietary or open source.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.141
question_text: Vincent discovered the vulnerability shown here when scanning a system on his network. What is the best action he can take to correct this vulnerability?

answer_choices.label: B. Add multifactor authentication to the FTP server.
C. Require the use of strong passwords.
D. Add an intrusion prevention system.

explanation: The File Transfer Protocol (FTP) is an insecure protocol that should not be used today. Vincent’s best course of action is to remove the FTP server and replace this insecure protocol with a secure alternative, such as FTPS.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.142
question_text: Which one of the following technologies would be most helpful in managing the configuration of devices brought into an organization under a BYOD computing approach?
explanation: Mobile device management (MDM) solutions manage the secure configuration of mobile devices, including those brought into the organization under a Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policy. Data loss prevention (DLP) systems do protect the organization against the accidental or intentional exfiltration of data, but they do not manage system configurations. Intrusion prevention systems (IPSs) do protect the organization against potentially malicious network traffic, but they do not manage system configurations. Firewalls do protect the organization against potentially malicious network traffic, but they do not manage system configurations.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.143
question_text: Which one of the following is not a common duty for an organization’s information security officer?

answer_choices.label: D. Communicating risks to the organization’s leaders

explanation: Information security officers are expected to communicate risks to leadership, are typically responsible for managing the information security budget, and often must respond to internal and external audit requests. Information security officers generally do not have direct operational responsibilities, such as configuring a firewall rule.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.144
question_text: Don would like to deploy a vulnerability scanner in his environment but does not have the budget to purchase a commercial solution. What scanning tool would best meet his needs?
explanation: OpenVAS is a free, open source vulnerability scanner, whereas Qualys, Nessus, and Nexpose are all commercial products.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.145
question_text: Helen recently worked with business leaders to determine the longest amount of time that her organization’s website may be down before affecting important business functions. What metric has she captured?
explanation: The maximum tolerable downtime (MTD) is the longest duration for which a resource may be unavailable before affecting a critical business function. The recovery time objective (RTO) is a similar measure identifying how quickly a system may be restored. The recovery point objective (RPO) is the amount of data loss that may occur during a recovery effort. The mean time between failures (MTBF) is the amount of time that elapses between failure events, on average.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.146
question_text: Tamara recently decided to purchase cyber‐liability insurance to cover her company’s costs in the event of a data breach. What risk management strategy is she pursuing?
explanation: Risk transference involves shifting the impact of a potential risk from the organization incurring the risk to another organization. Insurance is a common example of risk transference.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.147
question_text: Which one of the following elements of an information security policy framework does not contain mandatory requirements for employees?
explanation: Guidelines provide recommendations and best practices for achieving security objectives, but they are not mandatory. Policies provide high‐level statements of management intent and are mandatory for all employees. Standards provide mandatory configuration information and other control requirements for security programs. Required procedures provide mandatory step‐by‐step instructions for performing a security task.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.148
question_text: In an organization subject to the General Data Protection Regulation, which one of the following roles must be filled by someone granted a significant degree of independent authority to enforce privacy requirements?
explanation: The data protection officer (DPO) is an official role assigned by data processors subject to GDPR. The DPO is accountable to governmental authorities for the proper execution of an organization’s data protection and privacy program and must be granted the authority to carry out their work with autonomy.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.149
question_text: Bill recently accepted a new position as an information security manager after a career in other domains of risk management. His boss suggested to him that he quickly immerse himself in the world of information technology. What is the most important reason that an information security manager should be conversant in other areas of IT?

answer_choices.label: A. Knowledge of other IT domains will help Bill better understand the business.
B. Knowledge of other IT domains will provide Bill with the ability to modify system configurations.
C. Knowledge of other IT domains will help Bill design and implement security controls.
D. Knowledge of other IT domains would give Bill credibility with technical staff.

correct_answer_label: C. Knowledge of other IT domains will help Bill design and implement security controls.
explanation: The primary reason that Bill needs IT knowledge is to understand the impact of different technologies on security decisions, such as the design and implementation of security controls. While IT knowledge would certainly add to Bill’s credibility, the primary reason he needs IT knowledge is to understand the impact of different technologies on security decisions. Technical knowledge will not necessarily contribute to Bill’s knowledge of the organization’s business. As an information security manager, Bill should not find himself modifying system configurations.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.150
question_text: Which one of the following elements would not normally be included in a business case?

answer_choices.label: D. Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats

correct_answer_label: D. Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats
explanation: A business case normally includes a scope statement, a description of the strategic context, a cost analysis, an evaluation of alternatives, a project plan, and a management plan. It would not normally include a discussion of strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats. Those items would be included in a SWOT analysis.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.006
question_text: Ryan is a security risk analyst for an insurance company. He is currently examining a scenario in which a malicious hacker might use a SQL injection attack to deface a web server due to a missing patch in the company’s web application. In this scenario, what is the threat?
explanation: Risks are the combination of a threat and a vulnerability. Threats are the internal and external forces seeking to undermine security, such as the malicious hacker in this case. Vulnerabilities are the internal weaknesses that might allow a threat to succeed. In this case, the missing patch is the vulnerability. In this scenario, if the malicious hacker (threat) attempts a SQL injection attack against the unpatched server (vulnerability), the result could be website defacement.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.007
question_text: Frank is helping to develop a new chief information security officer (CISO) role at his organization, a health‐care provider. He is trying to determine the appropriate reporting lines for this position. The organization recently suffered a security breach, and the board has directed that the CISO report to someone with as much relevant decision‐making authority as possible. What would be the most common reporting relationship for a CISO?
explanation: CISOs have many different reporting relationships and Frank should select one that is appropriate for the culture of his organization. While a common reporting relationship is for the CISO to report to the chief information officer (CIO), the board in this case has directed that the CISO report to the person with as much relevant decision‐making as possible. Therefore the CISO should report to the chief executive officer (CEO).

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.008
question_text: Which one of the following would not commonly be available as an IaaS service offering?
explanation: Customer relationship management (CRM) packages offered in the cloud would be classified as software‐as‐a‐service (SaaS), since they are not infrastructure components. Storage, networking, and computing resources are all common IaaS offerings.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.009
question_text: Kim is designing a security awareness training program for her organization. What should be the primary goal of her efforts?

answer_choices.label: A. To improve communications between the security team and other users
B. To encourage the adoption of security technologies
C. To reduce the likelihood of a security incident
D. To increase awareness of security policy

correct_answer_label: C. To reduce the likelihood of a security incident
explanation: The primary goal of the awareness program should be to reduce the likelihood and/or impact of a security incident. The awareness program may include efforts to increase awareness of security policy, improve communications, and increase adoption of security technologies, but the primary goal of the program should be to reduce the likelihood and impact of a security incident.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.010
question_text: Gary is the information security manager for a large corporation, and he has received a request from a business unit to waive a security control requirement. Which one of the following conditions would make Gary most comfortable with approving the request?

answer_choices.label: A. Existence of a compensating control
B. Small amount of money involved
C. Statement from a senior executive that the waiver is important.
D. Length of time the control will be missing

correct_answer_label: A. Existence of a compensating control
explanation: Compensating controls use alternative means to meet the same security objective and are important considerations when making exceptions to security control standards. Gary should take the needs of the business into account, but an executive assertion should not be sufficient to make him comfortable with the request. While financial implications are important to Gary’s decision, there are nonfinancial risks that waiving a security control could create. Gary should consider the length of time that the exception will be in place, but the fact that a waiver is needed only for a short period of time may not be sufficient to mitigate the risk.

title: tb801931.CISMSG.pe1.011
question_text: Which one of the following individuals is normally responsible for fulfilling the operational data protection responsibilities delegated by senior management, such as validating data integrity, testing backups, and enforcing security policies?
explanation: The data custodian role is assigned to an individual who is responsible for implementing the security controls defined by policy and senior management. The data owner does bear ultimate responsibility for these tasks, but the data owner is typically a senior leader who delegates operational responsibility to a data custodian.
